title: talauma liliifera
intro: Talauma liliifera, also known as the Indian coral tree or liliifera, is a tropical tree species native to the Western Ghats of India. Its bark, leaves, and seeds possess medicinal properties, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing digestive issues. Talauma liliifera is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powders, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
